conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: The email must be written in the first person perspective.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "You may find this information helpful for your research."
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. The email must be written in the first person perspective. The email must include the phrase "You may find this information helpful for your research."
response:
Subject: Introduction - [Your Name] - Prospective Student/Enthusiast in [Professor's Field]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Your Year, e.g., second-year undergraduate, recent graduate, etc.] student at [Your University/Institution]. I am writing to you today because I have been deeply impressed by your work in [Mention Professor's Specific Field or a Recent Paper/Project]. I recently read your paper on [Specific Topic] and found your approach to [Specific Aspect of their work] particularly insightful.

My academic interests lie primarily in [Your Specific Area of Interest], and I am particularly drawn to the intersection of [Topic A] and [Topic B]. I am eager to pursue [Your Goal, e.g., graduate studies in this field, research opportunities, deeper understanding of X concept] and I believe your expertise in [Professor's Area] would be invaluable to my development.

I am currently [Briefly mention what you are doing now—e.g., working on a project about X, taking courses in Y, etc.]. As I continue to explore this area, I have been compiling some resources related to [Specific Sub-topic]. **You may find this information helpful for your research** as well, as it touches upon [briefly explain the relevance].

I would be incredibly grateful for the opportunity to briefly speak with you during your office hours or at another convenient time to introduce myself further and learn more about your current research and potential opportunities for involvement in your lab/department.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number (if applicable)]
[Your Email Address]
[Your Phone Number (Optional)]<turn|>
